title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
summary: The article highlights a new aluminum smelter planned in Oklahoma with a capacity of approximately 600,000 tons per year, which could nearly double current US domestic production. It notes that only six smelting operations remain in the US, with just four actively producing about 670,000 tons in 2024, accounting for less than 1% of global output. The US currently satisfies only about a third of its domestic demand even at full capacity.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost its production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on foreign exporters. Fox News senior correspondent Steve Harrigan reports on this decision as part of the latest developments in America's aluminum industry.

title: Steel Prices Surge To 4-Month Highs On Improving Macro-Eco ...
summary: Steel prices have reached a four-month high driven by an improving macroeconomic backdrop and China's plans to reform industrial policy. Chinese steel prices gained 3% in July, while US steel prices outperformed with a 20% increase this year due to a 50% import tariff.

title: US prices hold firm, imports remain appealing
summary: SMU's average price for domestic hot-rolled coil steel remained unchanged compared to the previous week, while offshore import prices continue to be appealing. The report also notes that SMU's June ferrous scrap market survey results are now available for premium members.

title: Steel fell to 3073 CNY/T on July 9, 2026
summary: Steel prices dropped to 3073 CNY/T on July 9, 2026, marking a 0.29% decline from the previous day. Over the past month, steel prices have fallen by 2.29% and are down 1.03% compared to the previous year.

title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop?
summary: Latest steel prices for July 2026 show hot-rolled coil at $1,140/ton, cold-rolled steel at $1,347/ton, and coated steel at $1,455/ton. The article discusses market trends and whether prices are expected to decline in the near future.

title: US job growth halves in June to 57,000
summary: US job growth slowed significantly in June, halving to 57,000, which may impact steel demand and market trends. This economic data is part of broader analysis on steel prices and market conditions.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: Boston Metal, a Bill Gates-backed startup, raised $50 million in January to develop a CO2-free steel process using molten oxide electrolysis. In Sweden, $3.99 billion is funding a new commercial hydrogen steel plant, while the technology aims to separate oxygen from iron ore using electricity, producing O2 instead of CO2.

title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: Northern Sweden will produce the first commercial volumes of near-zero emissions steel, leveraging bio-based and synthetic materials for carbon reduction and improved properties. Companies like GreenOre and Reclinker are monetizing waste streams, such as steel slag and construction waste, to create supplementary cementitious materials and recycled clinker.

title: Green Steel Technology – A $89 billion Revolution
summary: The global green technology and sustainability market for steel production is projected to reach over $89 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 20.9%. The U.S. Department of Energy announced an $83 million funding opportunity to reduce emissions in hard-to-decarbonize sectors, while the IEA envisions nearly all primary steel production using low-emissions technologies by 2050.

title: Steel News - Vallourec Decarbonization Strategy Certified
summary: Tubular solutions company Vallourec announced its decarbonization strategy has been certified by the Global Steel Climate Council (GSCC). This certification marks a significant step in the company's commitment to sustainable steel production and reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

title: Nickel prices hit 15-month high on robust sentiment
summary: LME nickel prices reached $18,045/t on January 6, marking a 7% increase from the previous day and exceeding $18,000/t for the first time in 15 months. Prices surged 20% from $14,430/t on December 17, 2025, in just 12 trading days, with Shanghai Futures Exchange also hitting its daily limit.

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Global nickel prices edged lower to $16,277.26 per ton on July 7, 2026, a 0.57% drop amid persistent oversupply pressures. The previous day saw a 0.56% advance to $16,443.63 per ton, driven by weak US nonfarm payroll data and China's 1 trillion yuan reverse repo injection.

title: Nickel fell to 16291.88 USD/T on July 9, 2026
summary: Nickel prices dropped to $16,291.88 per ton on July 9, 2026, representing a 0.78% decline from the previous day. The metal traded around $16,350 per tonne, with an overall 8.11% fall in recent performance.

title: Nickel Price - Nickel Rate in India Today Live
summary: LME suspended nickel trading for the day after prices saw a record run, with the current rate in India at 1,569.50 per kg. The suspension occurred amid significant price volatility and market uncertainty.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: Nickel futures are currently priced at $16,303.75, with the spot price at $16,301.50 and a previous close of $16,363.38. The price range for the day is between $16,201.50 and $16,437.75, showing moderate intraday volatility.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article investigates how optimizing nickel production can create a sustainable battery industry globally by implementing technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote energy efficiency. It emphasizes that enhancing stakeholder engagement and robust ESG disclosure standards are essential for transparency in the global nickel sector.

title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
summary: Nickel is identified as crucial for the net-zero transition with demand expected to grow 65% this decade, making sustainable production practices imperative. The report affirms that clean energy technologies are the fastest-growing sector for nickel demand and that scaling production must minimize environmental impact.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: This new series explores sustainability, treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production, starting with nickel ores. It asserts that nickel can be a sustainable material throughout its entire value chain—from mining to end-of-life—if all actors take responsibility.

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
summary: The global nickel market is undergoing rapid changes with innovations in mining and refining aimed at reducing production costs while enhancing environmental performance. These developments represent a critical path toward low-cost, sustainable nickel production solutions.
